items.fix: Always include `requires` and `build-backend`. Without it, `pip install .` falls back to legacy setuptools (or fails).
items.why: PEP 517/518 says: tell the front-end what to install before invoking the backend. No section → no contract.

items.fix: Use compatible ranges (`requests>=2.31,<3`) in `dependencies`; pin exactly in a lockfile.
items.why: Strict pins in libraries cause conflicts with other libs that pin different exact versions. Lockfiles are the right place for exact pins.

items.mistake: Hard-coding the version in two places.
items.fix: Use `dynamic = ['version']` and read it from `__init__.py` (or git via setuptools-scm/hatch-vcs).
items.why: Version drift between `pyproject.toml` and `__init__.py` is a classic release-day bug.

items.fix: Consolidate into `pyproject.toml`. Remove `setup.py` unless you need build-time Python (rare).
items.why: Multiple config sources confuse tools and humans; precedence rules differ per tool.
Without it, pip will happily install your package on Python 3.7, then your code breaks at runtime with mysterious errors.
